injected_bugs.title: SQL injection via template literal in user lookup
injected_bugs.description: User-controlled req.params.id is interpolated directly into SQL query string

description: Missing auth check on admin endpoint

injected_bugs.title: Missing authentication check on admin deleteUser endpoint
injected_bugs.description: Admin endpoint has no auth/authorization check — any caller can delete users

description: Null dereference when user profile is missing
diff:
--- a/src/services/profile.ts
+++ b/src/services/profile.ts
@@ -14,7 +14,7 @@ export async function getDisplayName(userId: string): Promise<string> {
-  const profile = await db.findOne({ userId })
-  return profile?.name ?? 'Anonymous'
+  const profile = await db.findOne({ userId })
+  return profile.name
 }

injected_bugs.title: Null dereference: profile.name accessed without null check
injected_bugs.description: db.findOne can return null, but profile.name is accessed without optional chaining or null guard

diff:
--- a/src/services/counter.ts
+++ b/src/services/counter.ts
@@ -8,8 +8,8 @@ export async function incrementPageViews(pageId: string): Promise<number> {
-  await db.query('UPDATE pages SET views = views + 1 WHERE id = $1', [pageId])
-  const result = await db.query('SELECT views FROM pages WHERE id = $1', [pageId])
+  const result = await db.query('SELECT views FROM pages WHERE id = $1', [pageId])
+  const newCount = result.rows[0].views + 1
+  await db.query('UPDATE pages SET views = $1 WHERE id = $2', [newCount, pageId])
   return result.rows[0].views
 }

injected_bugs.title: Race condition in page view counter: read-modify-write pattern
injected_bugs.description: SELECT then UPDATE is not atomic — concurrent requests will read the same value and produce incorrect counts. Should use UPDATE views = views + 1 atomically

description: Missing input validation allows negative prices

injected_bugs.title: Missing server-side price validation: negative prices accepted
injected_bugs.description: Price validation removed from server, trusting frontend only. Any API client can send negative prices, corrupting order totals

description: Unhandled promise rejection in background job

injected_bugs.title: Unhandled promise rejection in scheduleWelcomeEmail
injected_bugs.description: sendWelcomeEmail() returns a Promise that is not awaited or .catch()'d — any rejection will be an unhandled rejection, crashing Node.js in newer versions

description: N+1 query in product listing with categories

injected_bugs.title: N+1 query: category fetched individually per product in loop
injected_bugs.description: For 50 products this executes 51 SQL queries. Should use a JOIN or batch query with WHERE id = ANY($1)

description: Missing error handling in payment API call

injected_bugs.title: Missing error handling in chargeCard: Stripe errors crash caller
injected_bugs.description: stripe.charges.create throws on card decline or API errors. Without try/catch, payment failures become unhandled exceptions that crash the request handler

description: Dead code: unreachable return after throw

injected_bugs.title: Dead code: return statement after throw is unreachable
injected_bugs.description: The return statement on line 14 can never execute because the preceding throw always exits. Indicates a logic error or a refactoring artifact

description: Off-by-one in pagination: last page returns wrong results

injected_bugs.title: Off-by-one in pagination: page 1 skips first 20 results
injected_bugs.description: offset = page * limit gives offset=20 for page 1. Should be (page - 1) * limit so page 1 starts at offset 0
